title: MA (Politics and International Relations)
content: Bachelor Degree with minimum 50% marks or equivalent. Students having CUET Score will be given preference in merit 01 Develop the ability to analyze the complex aspects of international relations, and a knowledge of qualitative research methodologies 02 Develop in-depth understanding and critical analysis of international politics and world affairs. Become familiar with the latest research in the discipline alongside updated knowledge of world events as they unfold. 03 Demonstrate competence in communication and writing skills on key international relations theories, concepts, findings from qualitative and quantitative investigation. 04 Find careers in a variety of sectors, including the government and security services, think tanks, print and electronic media, non-governmental organizations, academia and multi-national corporations. 05 Develop the ability to think creatively and critically and contribute towards the betterment of humankind. 06 Connect theoretical constructs to pragmatic issues of the world politics 07 Engage in theoretical discourses in the field of international politics 08 Enable to gauge the links between human nature and world politics 09 Foster intellectual and positive analysis among students by engaging them in multiple discourse of world politics 10 Ability to assess the recent development of world politics in the age of artificial intelligence 01 Post-Graduates will be able to analyze and optimize key concepts, issues and theories of international relations in the field of Research. 02 Post-Graduates will develop the ability to work efficiently as leader/team member within the government and security services, think tanks, print and electronic media, non-governmental organizations, academia and multi-national corporations. 03 Post-Graduate students will develop a critical, analytical mind and problem-solving approach in the field of international politics to solve the pertaining problems of world affairs. 01 Will provide the student with an in depth and nuanced understanding of key concepts, issues and theories in international relations and role of various actors and stakeholders in global politics. 02 Students will be able to analyse the key drivers and determinants of states’ foreign policy and understand how international diplomacy is conducted. 03 Students will become familiar with various sources of knowledge on international relations.
